af_score_components.notes: Official MCP server (meilisearch-mcp) enabling agents to search and index directly. Excellent API documentation with OpenAPI spec and thorough guides. Async task system requires polling for write operations — agents must track task IDs for consistency. Well-designed error messages and scoped API keys (per-index, per-action). Retry guidance documented. Primary gotcha: all write ops are async tasks; hybrid search requires pre-configured embedder.

security_score_components.security_notes: Excellent key management: master key, admin keys, and scoped search-only keys with configurable expiry and per-index/action restrictions. GDPR compliance documented. Rust implementation means memory-safety by design. HTTPS enforced for Meilisearch Cloud; self-hosted production requires TLS configuration. API keys passed via environment variables with clear documentation on rotation.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Meilisearch Cloud has SLA; self-hosted is operator responsibility. Mature, semver-versioned project with good migration guides. Async task system with full idempotency support. Retry guidance documented. Known constraint: index settings changes trigger full reindexing (avoid during active queries). Maximum 1MB document size requires chunking strategy.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server providing persistent file-based memory for AI coding assistants across sessions. Core purpose is well-executed: read/write structured memory bank files with project isolation. MEMORY_BANK_ROOT misconfiguration silently stores files in wrong location — no validation error surfaced. No semantic search; agents must know file names. Path traversal prevention is a good security control. No auth required (local filesystem). Smithery-listed for easy installation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local filesystem tool with no network auth. MEMORY_BANK_ROOT env var provides the only access boundary — no authentication layer. Path traversal prevention prevents accessing files outside designated root, which is important. Directory-based project isolation is reasonable for the use case. TypeScript/Node with Smithery publishing; moderate hygiene. No secrets required to operate.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool with no SLA. Community project with reasonable stability for a TypeScript MCP server. Newer project with limited long-term track record. File operations are effectively idempotent (overwrite semantics). No documented retry behavior. Silent misconfiguration of MEMORY_BANK_ROOT is a reliability risk — files go to wrong location without error.

af_score_components.notes: Enterprise MCP control plane routing all MCP traffic through a governed single endpoint. Strong MCP focus with RBAC, credential vaulting, and OpenTelemetry observability. OAuth 2.1 setup is complex barrier for smaller teams. SUL (Sustainable Use License) means commercial users need paid license — important consideration. Virtual MCP strategies (full-context vs smart selection) need tuning per use case. Documentation present but not exhaustive for newer project.

security_score_components.security_notes: Best-in-class security for an MCP gateway: OAuth 2.1 with fine-grained permissions per workspace/project, RBAC, and a dedicated credential vault for secure token storage. Multi-tenant workspace isolation. OpenTelemetry enables full audit trail of tool access. Kubernetes-ready deployment with HTTPS. Bun/TypeScript/React stack with active development. This is the most security-mature MCP gateway evaluated.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted OSS with no formal SLA. Kubernetes-ready deployment provides infrastructure resilience. Newer project with active development — some API churn possible. OpenTelemetry observability aids incident response. No documented circuit-breaking for downstream MCP server failures. Multi-tenant workspace structure is non-trivial to change post-deployment.

af_score_components.notes: Open-source BI platform with REST API for managing questions, dashboards, and users. No MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Good documentation with API reference. Session tokens expire after 14 days — agents must re-authenticate before expiry. API is NOT formally versioned; updates can break automation between Metabase versions. Dashboard updates require knowing all existing card positions (no partial updates). Agent-hostile design: card IDs are integers auto-assigned that must be stored post-creation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS configurable for self-hosted deployments; recommended but not enforced by default. Session token + API key auth — no OAuth. 14-day session token expiry is reasonable. No fine-grained API scopes; permissions managed via Metabase's internal user/group system. GDPR compliance varies by deployment configuration. Embedding secret signed as JWT per request — correct pattern. Large Java/React codebase; dependency hygiene is moderate.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted OSS; Metabase Cloud offers SLA. Mature project but API intentionally not formally versioned — automation can break silently between Metabase upgrades. Session tokens require re-authentication before 14-day expiry. Partial idempotency support. No retry guidance documented. Dashboard update operations require fetching full state first, making operations fragile.

af_score_components.notes: Full-featured self-hosted MCP gateway with namespace grouping, per-endpoint rate limiting, multi-tenancy, tool overrides, and OpenAPI compatibility in one Docker Compose deployment. Per-endpoint rate limiting is a differentiator vs simpler gateways. Known bug: query parameter API key auth broken for SSE transport — agents MUST use Authorization header. In-memory rate limit counters not globally enforced across nodes in clustered deployments. Pre-allocated idle sessions reduce cold start latency.

security_score_components.security_notes: API key Bearer auth, OAuth per MCP spec 2025-06-18, session cookies for internal TRPC. Namespace-level isolation with per-namespace API keys provides reasonable multi-tenancy. Self-hosted; HTTPS depends on deployment config. pnpm/PostgreSQL stack. Known issue: query parameter API key auth is broken for SSE — must use Authorization header exclusively. Rate limit counters in-memory only (not globally enforced in cluster).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted OSS with no formal SLA. PostgreSQL-backed for state persistence. Active project. Pre-allocated idle sessions reduce cold start latency but consume resources. In-memory rate limit counters mean limits are per-node in clustered deployments. stdio MCP servers require mcp-proxy wrapper for SSE-only clients. No documented circuit-breaking for downstream server failures.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server controlling a Minecraft Java Edition bot via Mineflayer API. Niche but creative use case for embodied AI agents in a game environment. Requires Minecraft Java Edition running in LAN mode per session — manual startup each time. No persistence between sessions; bot state resets entirely on restart. Bot actions are non-idempotent (building blocks, moving, inventory changes). Tested on 1.21.8; compatibility with other versions not guaranteed. Not suitable for production agent infrastructure — purely experimental/educational.

security_score_components.security_notes: LAN-only tool with no TLS or authentication. Any LAN client can control the bot once configured — no access control. No scope limitations; full bot control granted. TypeScript/Node with Mineflayer dependency. No credentials required to operate. Security model is appropriate only for isolated local development/research environments — not suitable for any production or shared environment.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Requires Minecraft world manually started in LAN mode each session — inherently unreliable for automated workflows. No persistence; state resets on MCP server restart. Version compatibility tied to specific Minecraft release (1.21.8); Minecraft updates can break compatibility. Non-idempotent bot actions (building, moving, crafting) make error recovery difficult. Not designed for reliability in agent contexts.

af_score_components.notes: Enterprise document preprocessing platform with MCP server at localhost:8002/sse and REST API at localhost:8000. Impressive multi-format support: PDF, Word, Excel, PPT, images, audio, video in 109+ languages. JWT + API key + optional OIDC/SAML SSO is comprehensive but complex. Error message quality is low (50/100 per agent readiness data). Large PDFs auto-split but CPU-only deployment latency can be high. Requires Docker + optional CUDA GPU infrastructure. Documentation may lag in English.

security_score_components.security_notes: JWT access+refresh tokens with RBAC (admin/user roles). Optional SSO via OIDC/SAML for enterprise deployments. API key self-service for third-party integrations. HTTPS implied by JWT usage for production; self-hosted Docker deployment. Complex AI stack (PaddleOCR-VL, MinerU) with many dependencies makes hygiene harder to track. Standard JWT credential management.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted Docker with no SLA. Newer enterprise project with limited track record. Async document processing with no documented retry behavior. Processing latency on CPU-only deployments can be significant for large batches. GPU dependency for optimal performance adds infrastructure complexity. MCP server on different port (8002) from REST API (8000) requires careful configuration.

af_score_components.notes: Official MiniMax MCP server providing access to generative media APIs: TTS, voice cloning, image generation, video generation (Hailuo-02), and music. Well-defined tool set for creative media workflows. Critical gotcha: Global (minimax.io) vs China (minimaxi.com) endpoint mismatch gives cryptic 'invalid api key' error. Video generation is async — agents must poll for task completion separately. All outputs saved to local disk (MINIMAX_MCP_BASE_PATH). Costs accumulate in agentic retry loops — no rate limit clarity. Windows requires Developer Mode for stdio transport.

security_score_components.security_notes: Commercial API with enforced HTTPS. Simple API key authentication via MINIMAX_API_KEY env var. Separate regional API keys (Global vs China) adds configuration complexity but is appropriate for data residency. No fine-grained scope control — one API key grants access to all media generation capabilities. Official MiniMax-maintained Python package with standard dependency hygiene. API key handling via environment variables is correct.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Commercial API with implied SLA from MiniMax.ai. Versioned official SDK. Newer API offering — some endpoint/model instability possible. Video generation is async with task polling requirement; no documented max wait time or failure handling. No retry guidance documented. Costs can accumulate unexpectedly in agentic retry loops. Output limited to local disk — no cloud delivery default.

af_score_components.notes: S3-compatible open-source object storage with no MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). 100% S3 API compatible — any S3 SDK works by changing the endpoint URL, which dramatically lowers integration friction. Excellent documentation with cursor-based pagination and retry guidance. AWS SigV4 auth is standard and well-understood. AGPL-3.0 license requires Enterprise license for commercial/proprietary use. Default HTTP setup is insecure — TLS must be configured explicitly. Erasure coding config must be set at startup.

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS SigV4 auth with IAM-like bucket/object policies for fine-grained permission control. STS support for temporary credentials is excellent for agent use. SOC2 compliant. TLS must be explicitly configured in production (default is HTTP — insecure, documented clearly). Rust/Go implementation with mature security posture. Access key rotation is standard practice with documented patterns. High scope granularity via bucket and path-level policies.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: S3-compatible API stability is a core promise — breaking changes extremely rare. Erasure coding for data durability. SOC2 compliant. Full idempotency support. Retry guidance documented. Distributed MinIO requires quorum configuration planning. Erasure coding settings are immutable post-deployment. Self-hosted with no managed SLA, but mature operational tooling. S3 error code compatibility means well-understood error handling.

af_score_components.notes: French AI company offering efficient LLM models with OpenAI-compatible API format — agents configured for OpenAI can switch to Mistral by changing base URL and model name. No MCP server (has_mcp_server=false). Excellent documentation with OpenAPI spec. Simple Bearer token API key auth. European data residency for GDPR compliance. OpenAI compatibility is ~95% but not 100% — some OpenAI-specific features may not work. Model naming differs from OpenAI (document carefully). Two separate APIs: La Plateforme (agents) vs Le Chat (chat interface).

security_score_components.security_notes: ISO27001 certified and GDPR compliant with European data residency option. HTTPS enforced on all endpoints. Simple API key as Bearer token — industry standard pattern. No fine-grained scope control (single key = full access to all models). Python/JS SDK official packages with good hygiene. API keys via environment variables documented as best practice. No OAuth required — simplicity is a security win for agent integrations.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Commercial SLA for La Plateforme; status page available. Stable, versioned model API with OpenAI-compatible format guaranteeing broad ecosystem compatibility. Model versioning and deprecation policy documented. Full idempotency for completion calls. Retry guidance documented. Context window varies by model (Mistral Small 32K, Large 128K). System prompts may behave differently from other providers — test prompt engineering carefully.
